V-M-010.checks: Admin frontend builds and reflects current admin, Full Tunnel, system, analytics, and device-control surfaces without auth regression.
V-M-010.test-files: frontend-admin/src/main.tsx frontend-admin/src/components/Layout.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Login.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Users.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Devices.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Analytics.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Servers.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Plans.tsx frontend-admin/src/lib/api.ts frontend-admin/src/types/index.ts frontend-admin/src/index.css

levels.module-local: Run npm build after frontend-admin route, component, API binding, or type changes.
levels.wave-level: During Phase-24, pair the build with V-M-037 mobile smoke for /, /users, /devices, and /analytics.
levels.phase-level: Frontend-admin remains pass only if auth guard, route registration, and admin API bindings compile after compact UI changes.

module-checks.check-2: Layout, Dashboard, Users, Devices, Analytics, Servers, and Plans compile against current types.
module-checks.check-3: adminApi base path remains /api/v1 unless a separate backend/API migration is approved.
module-checks.check-4: Device API binding names remain getDevices, blockDevice, unblockDevice, rotateDevice, and revokeDevice.
module-checks.check-5: Phase-24 does not change auth/session storage behavior; M-039 owns that in Phase-25.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin login route and protected layout still build after compact UI changes.
scenarios.scenario-2: Dashboard, users, devices, servers, plans, and analytics routes compile against existing admin API bindings.
scenarios.scenario-3: A frontend-admin UI simplification changes auth, admin API base path, or backend endpoint shape without approval.
scenarios.scenario-4: A compact navigation change drops /devices, /users, or /analytics from the registered protected route surface.

observability.telemetry-1: Build evidence records command, exit code, and failing TypeScript/Vite output if any.
observability.telemetry-2: Do not record real admin tokens, refresh tokens, Authorization headers, or cookies in frontend-admin evidence.
